etc.purpose: Runtime configuration and dev-tooling assets for OpenMeter — not application source code. Houses seed-data pipelines (etc/seed) and a local OpenAPI proxy config (etc/wiretap) used exclusively for development, testing, and demos.

patterns.name: Environment-variable parameterisation with defaults
patterns.description: All runtime values (tokens, URLs, counts) are injected via ${VAR:default} so configs work out-of-the-box without manual edits and stay overridable in CI.

patterns.name: Benthos/Redpanda Connect pipeline skeleton
patterns.description: Each seed stream follows the identical input.generate + bloblang mapping + output.switch shape; only the bloblang body differs between streams.
patterns.example: input: { generate: { count: 0, interval: ... } } / pipeline: { processors: [{ bloblang: ... }] } / output: { switch: [...] }

patterns.name: CloudEvents 1.0 envelope
patterns.description: Every generated event must include specversion, id (UUID), type, source, subject, time, and datacontenttype. Dimensional attributes go inside data: {}, not at the envelope root.
patterns.example: { specversion: '1.0', id: uuid_v4(), type: 'api-requests', source: 'seeder', subject: ..., time: now(), data: { tokens: ... } }

patterns.name: Contract binding to generated v1 OpenAPI spec
patterns.description: etc/wiretap points contract at the generated api/openapi.yaml (v1 spec), never v3; Docker redirects use host.docker.internal, not localhost.

key_file_guides.role: Benthos pipeline entrypoint: imports all stream configs from etc/seed/streams/ and wires observability.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding new streams requires a corresponding entry here; a missing import silently skips that stream.

key_file_guides.role: Per-domain generators (api-requests, prompt-tokens, workload-runtime) reusing the seed skeleton.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Copy the skeleton verbatim for new event types; deviating from the input.generate + output.switch shape breaks the pipeline, and dimensions must stay inside data: {}.

key_file_guides.role: Shared Benthos metrics/tracing config included by seed.yaml.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not duplicate in individual stream files.

key_file_guides.role: Wiretap proxy config for Docker-local v1 API contract validation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must target api/openapi.yaml (v1), not api/v3/openapi.yaml; redirectURL must use host.docker.internal.

anti_patterns: Hardcoding OPENMETER_TOKEN or OPENMETER_BASE_URL — always use ${VAR:default}.
Setting count > 0 in generate — seed streams must run indefinitely (count: 0 means infinite).
Putting dimensional attributes outside data: {} — they belong in data, not at the CloudEvent root.
Pointing the wiretap contract at api/v3/openapi.yaml — wiretap here validates v1 traffic only.
Using localhost instead of host.docker.internal for redirectURL in the wiretap Docker config.

decisions.decision: Seed streams share an identical Benthos skeleton with only the bloblang mapping varying.
decisions.rationale: Keeps all streams structurally uniform so operators can read, copy, and modify any stream without learning a new pipeline shape.

decisions.decision: Subject space bounded by SEEDER_SUBJECT_COUNT modulo rather than purely random UUIDs.
decisions.rationale: Produces a realistic bounded set of subjects (mimicking real tenants) instead of ever-growing cardinality that would skew meter aggregations in demos.

test.purpose: Integration and cross-domain test suites that require a real PostgreSQL database. Each child owns one domain or cross-domain scenario; all wire services from raw package constructors (never app/common) to avoid import cycles.

patterns.name: BaseSuite embedding for shared billing infrastructure
patterns.description: Tests needing the full billing stack embed test/billing.BaseSuite (optionally + SubscriptionMixin). BaseSuite provisions Atlas migrations, a unique namespace, MockStreamingConnector, and pkg/clock control.
patterns.example: type MyTestSuite struct { billingtest.BaseSuite }; // then access s.BillingService, s.MockStreamingConnector

patterns.name: TestEnv interface for service access
patterns.description: test/notification, test/app, and test/customer expose a TestEnv interface rather than service structs directly, hiding wiring details from test methods.

patterns.name: Unique namespace per test method
patterns.description: Each test method generates a unique namespace (ULID or function-name-based via setupNamespace) to prevent FK constraint violations when tests share a database.
patterns.example: ns := ulid.Make().String() // or setupNamespace(t) returning a unique string

patterns.name: Raw package constructors only — no app/common imports
patterns.description: All test wiring constructs services from direct constructors (adapter.New, service.New), not from Wire provider sets in app/common, to avoid import cycles with domain testutils.

patterns.name: clock.SetTime + defer clock.ResetTime for deterministic time
patterns.description: Time-dependent tests call pkg/clock.SetTime at the start and defer clock.ResetTime() to restore global state for parallel tests.

patterns.name: t.Context() over context.Background()
patterns.description: All new test code uses t.Context() (or s.T().Context()) so context cancellation is tied to the test harness lifecycle.

key_file_guides.role: BaseSuite: provisions Atlas migrations, unique namespace, MockStreamingConnector, billing service stack. Extended by most billing tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always use BaseSuite rather than manually provisioning these — duplicating setup leads to subtle test-isolation bugs.

key_file_guides.role: SubscriptionMixin: adds subscription+plan wiring on top of BaseSuite for billing+subscription flows.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use SubscriptionMixin when a test needs both billing and subscription services; do not wire subscription separately.

key_file_guides.role: Wires the full notification stack including a real Svix client (requires SVIX_HOST env var).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not create a second TestEnv inside a test method — each starts goroutines and DB connections. Always defer env.Close().

key_file_guides.role: Entry point for credits/charges integration tests; uses chargestestutils.NewServices as the canonical charges stack builder.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use CreateLedgerBackedCustomer for tests requiring ledger accounts, not BaseSuite.CreateTestCustomer.

key_file_guides.role: Wires all subscription+billing deps via direct constructors and subscriptiontestutils.SetupDBDeps; shared testDeps used by all scenario files.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Hardcoded namespace 'test-namespace' — do not use a different namespace in this package; create a billing profile before invoice generation.

key_file_guides.role: TestEnv for app integration tests; wires billing via InitBillingService helper without app/common.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always call setupNamespace(t) per test method in AppHandlerTestSuite — never share a namespace; defer stripeClient.Restore() in stripe sub-tests.

key_file_guides.role: Child: grant burn-down/expiry/voiding/reset regression scenarios; the test/entitlement parent holds only CLAUDE.md.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No .go source belongs directly in test/entitlement — all code lives in regression/.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common or any Wire provider set in test files — always wire from raw package constructors to avoid import cycles.
Using context.Background() where t.Context() or s.T().Context() is available.
Reusing the same namespace, subject key, or customer key across test functions in the same package — causes unique/FK constraint violations.
Omitting defer clock.ResetTime() after clock.SetTime() — leaves the global clock dirty for subsequent parallel tests.
Accessing adapter methods directly when the service covers the same operation — bypasses the state machine and validation.

decisions.decision: Integration tests live in test/* rather than co-located with production code.
decisions.rationale: Cross-domain tests import multiple openmeter/* packages; a neutral test/* tree prevents import cycles and makes the cross-domain dependency explicit.

decisions.decision: Atlas migrations run by default in BaseSuite (not ent.Schema.Create).
decisions.rationale: Running Atlas migrations validates migration files against the live schema, catching drift before CI. TEST_DISABLE_ATLAS exists only as an escape hatch.

decisions.decision: Scenario-based test files in test/subscription rather than table-driven tests.
decisions.rationale: Subscription lifecycle scenarios are complex multi-step flows where numbered step comments document the flow more clearly than a flat input/output table.

code_examples.scenario: Extending BaseSuite for a billing+subscription integration test
